name: feature-cell
role: Single feature primitive — icon (or numeral, or glyph) above a heading above a short body paragraph, optionally with a trailing link or chip. The atomic unit composed into feature-grid. Four variants — compact (chip-sized), default (3-up grid cell), wide (2-up rich cell with longer body), feature (bento centerpiece with display headline). Designed for marketing surfaces but reused on docs landing pages, pricing-tier feature lists, and onboarding.

semanticChildren: <h3> or <h4> — feature heading (heading-level determined by surrounding context; the grid usually sits inside a <section> with its own <h2>)
— feature body (2–3 sentences max; bento variant allows up to 4)
<svg|icon|figure> — optional leading icon / numeral / glyph (decorative; aria-hidden=true unless functional)

variants.compact: icon + 1-line label only; used in 6-up trust-bar-style strips. Type-size-xs/sm. No body paragraph.
variants.default: icon + heading + 2-3 sentence body. Type-size-md heading. Used in 3-up marketing rows.
variants.wide: icon + heading + 3-4 sentence body + optional small chip cluster. Used in 2-up rich cells.
variants.feature: display-tier heading + 4-5 sentence body + optional CTA + optional embedded media. Used as bento centerpiece (single full-width or 2-row tall cell).

leadingPattern.icon: leading SVG via <icon> primitive at size-md or size-lg; coloured by --leading-color (defaults to --accent)
leadingPattern.numeral: monospaced numeral (01, 02, 03 …) at type-size-lg; used in 'three-step' feature rows
leadingPattern.glyph: single character glyph (▲ ◆ ○) at type-size-xl; used in editorial-register marketing
leadingPattern.none: skip leading element entirely; used when feature heading itself carries the visual weight

meta.interactiveBehavior: when interactive=true the entire <article> becomes tabbable + Enter/Space activates as if the trailing link were clicked. Mouse hover lifts shadow + translates 1px. Used on linkable feature cards (e.g. /features/<slug> destinations).
meta.loadingPattern: skeleton lines: 1 line for heading, 2-3 lines for body; matches the variant's expected line count. Leading icon becomes a skeleton circle.
meta.selectedPattern: border-color: --accent + ring-shadow 0 0 0 1px --accent; used when the cell represents a selected option in a comparison-grid context

ariaPatterns.decorativeIcon: aria-hidden='true' on the leading SVG when it's purely decorative; if the icon is the only thing conveying meaning, provide an aria-label on the article or visible text equivalent
ariaPatterns.interactiveCard: if interactive=true and the entire card is clickable, set role='link' (or use <a> wrapping the contents); preserve text-content focus order

name: feature-grid
role: Composition primitive for laying out multiple feature-cells. Renders responsive grid — 1-col mobile / 2-col tablet / 3-col desktop by default. Five canonical layout modes — three-up (3 default cells), four-tile (2×2 dense), six-tile (2×3 dense compact cells), bento (irregular spans where one feature cell takes 2×2 and others fill around it), steps (numbered horizontal flow on desktop, vertical on mobile). Handles empty / sparse / dense / loading / error states at the grid level.

layouts.three-up: grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr) at ≥768px; repeat(2, 1fr) at 480–767px; 1fr below 480px. Used for: 3 marketing benefits, 3 pricing-tier feature lists, 3 product pillars.
layouts.four-tile: grid-template-columns: repeat(2, 1fr); 2×2 layout at ≥640px; stacks at <640px. Used for: 4 product features, 4 use cases.
layouts.six-tile: grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr) at ≥1024px; repeat(2, 1fr) at 640–1023px; 1fr below 640px. Used for: 6 capabilities, 6 integrations, 6 services.
layouts.bento: irregular grid using grid-template-areas. One 'hero' cell spans 2 columns × 2 rows; remaining cells fill the surrounding L-shape. Used for: marketing hero-with-supporting-features, dashboard summary widgets.
layouts.steps: horizontal flex at ≥768px with numbered connectors between cells; vertical stack at <768px. Used for: 3-step onboarding, how-it-works sequences.

stateBehavior.empty: render empty-state element with optional CTA; 1-column layout with dashed border
stateBehavior.loading: render N skeleton feature-cells matching expected count at layout's expected density
stateBehavior.error: render error-state with critical-tinted surface + retry action

responsiveBreakpoints.mobile: <480px — single column for all layouts except 'steps' (which stays vertical)
responsiveBreakpoints.tablet: 480px–1023px — 2 columns for most layouts; bento collapses to 2-column linear
responsiveBreakpoints.desktop: ≥1024px — full layout as documented per layout type
